title: Refactor and simplify python builds
body:
* podman RPM will install podman and pypodman eggs
* pypodman namespaced in site-packages
* version numbers pulled from requirements.txt
* Makefiles updated to run all operations with PYTHON when set
* Fixed bugs with supporting python3.4

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: I have not looked at the code, but if the podman package installs the python code, doesn't this mean podman will require python?  Definitely not something we want.

@jwhonce btw, doesn't look like the spec successfully builds. With your spec (and also with the fedora dist-git spec), I see: 

-----------------------------------------------------
/usr/bin/make -C contrib/python/podman install
make[1]: Entering directory '/home/lsm5/repositories/libpod/contrib/spec/BUILD/libpod-341364dc5bf716e328295659bb9e541bcf877297/contrib/python/podman'
/usr/bin/python3 setup.py install
running install
error: can't create or remove files in install directory

The following error occurred while trying to add or remove files in the
installation directory:

    [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/test-easy-install-30254.write-test'

The installation directory you specified (via --install-dir, --prefix, or
the distutils default setting) was:

    /usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/

Perhaps your account does not have write access to this directory?  If the
installation directory is a system-owned directory, you may need to sign in
as the administrator or "root" account.  If you do not have administrative
access to this machine, you may wish to choose a different installation
directory, preferably one that is listed in your PYTHONPATH environment
variable.

For information on other options, you may wish to consult the
documentation at:

  https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/easy_install.html

Please make the appropriate changes for your system and try again.

make[1]: *** [Makefile:17: install] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/lsm5/repositories/libpod/contrib/spec/BUILD/libpod-341364dc5bf716e328295659bb9e541bcf877297/contrib/python/podman'
make: *** [Makefile:245: install.python] Error 2
error: Bad exit status from /var/tmp/rpm-tmp.yEkgAp (%install)


RPM build errors:
    Bad exit status from /var/tmp/rpm-tmp.yEkgAp (%install)
-----------------------------------------------------

@lsm5 the site-package directory is selected based on the version of the python command you are running, so you should be running 3.6 given that output.  Currently the Makefiles assume you are root to do an install.  

Seeing @rhatdan 's comment, I'll add another commit to this break out a python-podman rpm from the podman rpm.  /cc @baude

> the site-package directory is selected based on the version of the python command you are running, so you should be running 3.6 given that output. Currently the Makefiles assume you are root to do an install.

So, on fedora 28, there's just 1 python3 and that's python 3.6. The rpm spec upstream uses `python3 setup.py install --root %{buildroot}` . Fedora's dist-git also uses `%py3_install` which is pretty much the same as setup.py install --root %{buildroot}, so neither of those should need root. Anyway, let me look further and get back.

@lsm5 Your error report starts with "/usr/bin/make -C contrib/python/podman install"

which on this PR installs in the system libraries not BUILDROOT so would require root.  On your examples what is BUILDROOT 's value?

@jwhonce The error I posted above was with your spec file in contrib/spec/ . It uses `--root %{buildroot}`. Are you able to get a successful `rpmbuild -ba podman.spec` using that spec file and with a non-root user?
@rhatdan This PR is step one for removing python as a dependency of podman.
@jwhonce I was thinking of this being only a specfile issue, not requiring code changes.

:pushpin: Commit 6819d4a has been approved by `rhatdan`

@lsm5 rhel7 does not work.  We either need varlink support for later versions of python or SCL python  support for our RPM.
